methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $8.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Reflection AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $8B

private.name: Together AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series A), post-money $3.3B

data.narrative:
CuspAI is a Series A-stage artificial intelligence and software company that has raised over $100M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 private frontier-tech firms and 1 public company (Palantir, ticker PLTR), including peers at Series A and Series B stages such as Reflection AI ($8.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), Together AI ($3.3B), and Black Forest Labs ($3.2B).

Two valuation methods were applied. The stage-progression approach uses CuspAI's $100M total raised as a baseline value proxy and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding an implied value of $200M. The peer-median method identifies four same-sector, same-or-adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $4.5B (range: $3.2B–$8.0B), reflecting current market pricing for AI-stage companies at similar development phases.

These methods produce a wide triangulated range. The low-end estimate is $170M, driven by the conservative stage-progression method. The median across both approaches is $2.3B, while the high end reaches $5.1B, anchored by peer multiples. Key uncertainties include whether CuspAI's capital efficiency and burn trajectory align with early-stage or late-stage peers, and whether the peer set adequately reflects product maturity, customer traction, and defensibility relative to the target.
